observations.recommendation:
1.10. We reiterated our recommendation that:
1.10.1. Management, thru the Inventory Committee prioritize the conduct of
the physical count of all properties and submit to the auditor the Report
on Physical Count of PPE/Inventory report, to be done annually
henceforth;
1.10.2. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required to
maintain and subsequently update the Ledger Cards and Stock and
Property Cards in order that the reconciliation of the physical count
against book balances can be facilitated; and
1.10.3. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the
accounting and property records and make the necessary adjustments
so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial
statements.

2.8. We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of the various PPE accounts
under the Trust Fund to the General Fund. Corresponding depreciation
expenses should then be properly recorded in the books.

3.9. We reiterated our recommendation that:
3.9.1. The OIC – Municipal Agriculturist, conduct an inventory of breeding
stocks and plants and trees as of the end of each year and thereafter
render a report including the fair market value of these items to the
Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of the LGU;
3.9.2. The OIC – Municipal Agriculturist henceforth keep a systematic record
of the Municipality’s breeding stocks and trees, reporting births, deaths
and disposal, and in case of death and disposal, facilitate request for
relief of accountability in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386
dated October 20, 1992 to appropriately drop the amount from the
books; and
3.9.3. The Municipal Accountant disclose the composition of the Biological
Assets account in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance
with IPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect the
fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

4.8. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed that the Local
Chief Executive:
4.8.1. Require the conduct of physical count of inventories every semester and
submit a report thereon to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31,
and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively;
4.8.2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant
weekly the SSMI supported by RIS as basis for recording the utilized
inventory items in the books, and
4.8.3. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile the accounting and inventory records and make the necessary
adjustments so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial
statements.

5.7. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed:
5.7.1. That the Municipal Accountant, coordinate with GSIS, Pag-IBIG, and
PhilHealth to determine the outstanding premium contributions of each
employee in their records;
5.7.2. To ensure that mandatory contributions are remitted in accordance
with the provisions of R.A. No. 8291, R.A. No. 9679, and R.A. No. 7875
as amended by R.A. No. 9241 and R.A. No. 10606 and its IRRs to avoid
the incurrence of interest and penalties; and
5.7.3. To provide justification for the non-remittance. Any additional interest
charges and penalties that may be imposed for late remittances shall be
shouldered by the responsible officers.

6.11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
6.11.1. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the
difference between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances
totaling ₱40,038,710.14 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation,
together with the Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the
Basic and Special Education Tax with the balances of the RPT
Receivables and SET Receivables accounts to avoid accumulation of
unreconciled differences; and
6.11.2. The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly
certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the
current year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET
Receivable accounts in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

7.6. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
strictly comply with Section 69 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 by
ensuring that all collections are deposited intact with the authorized depository
bank at the end of each day or within the prescribed period.

7.7. Additionally, internal controls should be strengthened by implementing
regular monitoring, surprise cash inspections to prevent fund
misappropriation and mitigate the risk of loss, and to consider appropriate
disciplinary actions for any repeated violations.

8.16. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
8.16.1. Conduct a thorough investigation into the circumstances surrounding
the BAC’s acceptance of the non-compliant bid and the failure to
disqualify the bidder who submitted another entity’s SLCC;
8.16.2. Hold the BAC and TWG members including other personnel involved
in the procurement transaction accountable for their actions by
enforcing suitable sanctions or disciplinary measures in accordance
with established procurement regulations and relevant municipal
administrative policies;
8.16.3. Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly adhere to
the eligibility requirements and other provisions of RA No. 9184 in all
future procurements;
8.16.4. Implement sanctions for bidders who submit non-compliant bids or
falsified documents to deter unethical practices and maintain the
integrity of the bidding process; and
8.16.5. Allow BAC and TWG members and other personnel involved in the
procurement process to attend regular training and capacity-building
activities to enhance their understanding of procurement rules and
regulations and ensure proper implementation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile the
records per books with the results of the bank confirmation and to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to present the correct
balance of the Cash in Bank account as of year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant
agreed to reconcile their records monthly so that any error in recording can be
adjusted and corrected immediately.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to examine the nature
of the deposits that are not in the records of the LGU and furnish the
Municipal Accountant with the complete list of the depository accounts to
update the subsidiary ledger of the Cash in Bank account. If the purpose of the
deposit is already served, close the account and transfer the deposit to the main
depository account of the General Fund or return the deposit to the funding
agency in the case of a Trust Fund.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to update the
records of the depository accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting entries
in the books of accounts to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank
account as of year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discontinue the placement in
time deposits of funds that are not determined to be idle and henceforth secure
prior authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to invest idle funds in time
deposit accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cancel the stale checks and
send written notices to the respective payees of the cancellation thereof.

We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the age of
unclaimed checks and send written notices to the payees at least one month
before the checks become stale and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant
for the necessary adjustments in the books of account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate a separate
liquidating officer to ensure that all collections are deposited intact to the
authorized government depository bank within the timeframe prescribed by
Section 32 of the NGAS to avoid any possible loss or misuse of government
funds.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the disbursing
officer to immediately liquidate her cash advances, reconcile her record with
the accounting records, and direct the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor
the granting and settlement of cash advances.

We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to appoint or
designate new disbursing officers until the incumbent disbursing officer has
fully liquidated her cash advances.

In addition, we recommended and Management agreed to strictly follow the
provisions outlined in COA Circular 97-022 to prevent government funds from
possible loss through misapplication.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce the collection of
delinquent RPT by availing of the remedies in collecting delinquent RPT
provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160, which is by administrative
action through levy on real property or judicial action.

We recommended and the BAC members, its TWG, and the Secretariat agreed
that payment of honoraria be limited to collections from procurement
activities enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-3
dated November 29, 2007, or from the agency’s savings for the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairman and the
Municipal Accountant ensure that honoraria to be paid to members of the
BAC, its TWG, and Secretariat are within the allowable limit of 25 per cent of
the basic monthly salary.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile the
difference between the RPT and SET Receivables account balances
totaling ₱68,459,106.09 and henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation,
together with the Municipal Assessor, of the total collectibles from the
Basic and Special Education Tax with the balances of the RPT Receivables
and SET Receivables accounts to avoid accumulation of unreconciled
differences;

b. The Municipal Treasurer maintain Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card and furnish the Municipal Accountant a
duly certified list of taxpayers with the amount due and collectible for the
current year as basis for recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable
accounts in compliance with Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I; and

c. The Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for accounts RPT
Receivable and SET Receivable in accordance with Section 10 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce collection of
delinquent real property taxes by conducting intensive tax campaign in every
barangay. We further recommended that the Municipality avail of the remedies
in collecting delinquent real property taxes provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA
No. 7160.

We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the Municipal Planning
and Development Coordinator, evaluate the feasibility and closely
monitor the implementation of the different PPAs of the Municipality;

b. The Municipal Mayor evaluate the number of personnel assigned in the
Municipal Engineering Office, taking into consideration their profiles
and technical qualifications; and

c. Management consider implementing some of the projects by contract.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide complete
and accurate information on the Quarterly Report on the Status of PPAs to ensure
their proper monitoring in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor the following
documents to substantiate the grant of Hazard Pay:

a. Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious Diseases
Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace;

b. Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the
period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well
as the entitlement of the COVID-19 Hazard Pay; and

c. The lacking documents noted in Annex 17.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to avoid using
the cash advance for petty operating expenses to pay for regular and recurring expenses.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions COA
Circular No. 2012-001 as to the documentary requirements necessary in order to
fully establish the validity of claims against government funds.

Lastly, the reimbursements of ₱690.00 and ₱3,999.00 to Mr. Julius Bolo
totaling ₱4,689.00 is deemed irregular for utilizing government funds for private
purpose which is contrary to Section 4(2) of P.D. No. 1445, and is therefore
disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management demand, in writing, the Acting DO to
immediately liquidate previous cash advances before any additional cash
advances be granted to her, otherwise impose proper sanction such as withholding
of her salary pursuant to Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February
10, 1997. Subsequently, the Acting DO should immediately liquidate any cash
advances granted to her as soon as the purpose of which it was granted has been
served.

We also recommended that Management require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents of the DVs
prior to the approval for payment of the Municipal Mayor, pursuant to Section
4, paragraphs 5 and 6 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint or designate
additional DO to avoid the granting of multiple cash advances to the incumbent
DO.

We recommended that Management require the accountable officers
handling government funds and property to post bond in the amount
corresponding to their total cash and property accountability in order to protect
the interest of the Municipality.

Henceforth, no Accountable Officer shall be allowed to perform their
duties and responsibilities with expired fidelity bonds and ensure that cash
advances granted to accountable officers do not exceed the maximum
accountability covered by their fidelity bonds.

In addition, submit a copy of the Confirmation Letter from the Bureau of
the Treasury to the Auditor for information and filing.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be instructed to fast-
track the preparation of the financial statements and submit them to our office at
the soonest possible time.

We further recommended that Management closely monitor the strict
adherence to the requirements of pertinent laws and regulation relating to the
submission of financial statements and other accounting reports, and cause the
suspension of payment of salaries of employees responsible for the delayed
submission or non-submission until the same shall be complied.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all department
heads concerned to submit their respective duly accomplished AAPSI and assign
a personnel to consolidate the same for submission to the Auditor at the soonest
possible time, and within 60 days upon receipt of the AARs for the succeeding
years.

We further recommended that pertinent documents to support the Actions
Taken should also be submitted to facilitate our validation of the responses.

observations.reason: Demand for settlement was not acted upon; sanctions were not imposed.
Issuance of official receipt of the Republic not provided; matter referred.
LGU's efforts were focused on addressing the COVID-19 pandemic; physical count not prioritized.
Current projects were prioritized; engineer unable to prepare revised program and as-built plans.
Collecting officers did not maintain cashbooks; reconciliation not done.
Officials and personnel refused to refund; matter to be verified.
Internal Audit Service was created but functions were not specifically defined; many members retired.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. Management create an inventory committee to conduct the physical
count of inventories and properties, and that RPCI and RPCPPE be
submitted semestrally and annually, respectively;
b. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer be required
to update the Ledger Cards and Stock and Property Cards in order
that the reconciliation of the physical count against book balances can
be facilitated; and
c. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile the
accounting and property records, and make the necessary adjustments
so that the accounts can be fairly presented in the financial statements.
In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere to the
provisions of Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, specifically that
regular purchases be coursed thru the inventory account and issuances thereof
shall be charged to their appropriate expense accounts as they take place and have
the Municipal Treasurer monitor these purchases in lieu of the General Services
Officer.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
and Acting Municipal Treasurer to strictly adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method
in accounting for inventory items to ensure proper accounting and promote better
control in the receipt and utilization thereof. Likewise, we recommended that
periodic inventory counting of supplies be conducted so the correct balances are
established which will serve as basis for the Municipal Accountant to draw journal
entry voucher for the adjustment of the inventory accounts of the Municipality.
Finally, we recommended that the Office of the Municipal Mayor propose
the creation of the GSO to the Saangguniang Bayan and appoint a permanent
General Services Officer in accordance with the qualification cited in Section
490(a) of R.A. No. 7160 and the Civil Service laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management verify that projects to be funded by
the 20% DF and subsequent disbursements made thereof conform to DILG-DBM
JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order that the desired socio-economic
development and environmental management outcomes geared mainly towards
the creation of jobs, promotion of economic growth and eradication of poverty, as
envisioned by the national government, may be achieved. Moreover, advise the
MPDC Officer to prepare a revised AIP for the 20% DF for the realignment of
those appropriations which were not properly chargeable thereto.

We recommended that the Management
a. render proper accounting of the difference noted from the purchased and
distributed food items;
b. install sound internal control mechanism in the repacking of goods to
monitor the release of items for repacking and account the packed relief
items to ensure that these are safeguarded from possible theft, loss and
wastage;
c. require the MDRRMO or property officer to prepare reports on the
inventory and monitoring of all procured and donated supplies, materials,
equipment, and relief goods in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-
002 dated April 15, 2014; and
d. direct the Municipal Accountant to record all purchases of supplies,
medicines, and relief goods for distribution in accordance with the
aforementioned COA Circular.

We recommended that (a) the MSWDO submit a written explanation on
the lapses and deficiencies enumerated in this observation; and (b) henceforth,
require Management to establish a system in the distribution of SAP and similar
cash assistance programs wherein the guidelines issued by the DSWD are followed
and that proper validation and appropriate profiling of beneficiaries are
performed before disbursement.

We recommended that Management provide justification through
written explanation why the POs did not indicate the required warranty clause
and/or provide additional documentary requirements to support the
disbursments.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer
to submit to the Auditor the following documents to substantiate the grant of SRA:
a) Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious
Diseases Resolution of the Risk Certification in the workplace;
b) Authority from the head of agency/office to render service during the
period of the ECQ stating the prescribed official working hours as well
as the entitlement of the SRA;
c) Individual approved daily time record or daily report of attendance
indicating the dates and time they physically reported for work; and
d) Daily status/monitoring report on COVID-19 patients, PUIs or PUMs
quarantined in the Municipality’s COVID-19 facility, in the homes, and
in other isolation facilities covering the same period of the ECQ.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit justification to prove reasonableness for the procurement of
the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH. Henceforth, procure
medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the SRP set by appropriate
government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the
government.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require end-user units to prepare their respective PPMPs in
accordance with Section 7 of RA No. 9184 using the suggested format
by the GPPB duly posted in its website.
b. Require the Budget Office to thoroughly evaluate and review the
submitted PPMPs in terms of amount and coherence with the proposed
budget of the LGU.
c. Require the BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the
PPMPs into a comprehensive, workable, and realistic APP, which will
serve as basis for procurement, in accordance to the applicable
provisions of RA No. 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB
Circular No. 07-2015.
d. Submit the duly approved copy of the APP to the GPPB in accordance
with AO No. 46, series of 2015

We recommended that Management submit the updated or revised APP,
in the prescribed format, duly approved and effecting the actual method of
procurement undertaken pursuant to the process and documentary requirements
as provided in GPPB Circular 01-2020 relative to procurements under the
Bayanihan Act for review and evaluation by the audit team. We also
recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer justify the payment
of the transactions despite the absence the original copy of the notarized Omnibus
Sworn Statement for those procurements with ABC above ₱500,000.00.

We recommended that Management provide a written explanation to
justify the non-posting in the online GPPB portal, appropriate documents relative
to the procurement projects undertaken under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.
We also recommended that Management comply with future posting
requirements for accountability and transparency.

We recommended that Management require the Chairperson and
members of the Bids and Awards Committee to:
a. always indicate in the APP the appropriate mode of procurement
b. prepare a BAC resolution recommending and justifying to the HoPE
the use of alternative mode of procurement with subsequent approval
of the HoPE or his duly authorized representative
c. strictly comply with the procurement process mandated by the IRR of
R.A. No. 9184.
Moreover, we recommended that Management submit the lacking
required documents to the Office of the Auditor, and comply with the
documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 for future
disbursments.

We recommended that Management advise the Municipal Budget Officer
to maintain the Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations. The
format of these forms can be referred to in Annexes 13, 14, 15 and 16 of Volume
II of the MNGAS. At the back of these forms are instructions on how it shall be
accomplished.

We recommended that Management instruct the LDRRM Officer to
include PPAs chargeable against the prior years unexpended LDRRMF in the
annual LDRRMFIP of the current year pursuant to NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC
No. 2013-1 and the same be prepared in adherence to the format prescribed under
Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002,

We recommended that Management:
a. immediately stop from utilizing the LDRRMF for PPAs which are not
properly chargeable to the Fund;
b. henceforth, the PPAs to be included in the annual LDRRMF Investment
Plan should conform to the PPAs enumerated in NDRRMC-DBM-DILG
JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
c. the amount of ₱192,920.51 representing a portion of the LDRRMF
appropriations spent for fuel, oil and lubricants, which are not properly
chargeable thereto, should be refunded from the appropriations of the GF
Proper.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer prepare the Monthly Report
on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly reconciled with the accounting
records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, and submit the report
to the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that Management ensure that the rules and regulations
on procurement as provided under R.A. 9184, its IRR, and related issuances of
the Government Procurement Policy Board are properly observed to obtain the
most advantageous price for the government.

Application for GAD database was reallocated to address COVID-related PPAs.
The open dump site was converted into a controlled dump site but not a full-scale sanitary landfill; allocation and enforcement still ongoing.
On-going payment; attention called and partial refund made.
Some officers and employees settled their accountabilities; others separated from service.
The practice has been stopped; verbal warning issued.
Difference still exists; Municipal Accountant assigned an employee to focus on reconciliation.
Preparation of the liquidation report was not prioritized; documents already with Municipal Accountant.
There are still unpaid balances for some employees.
The Certified List of Taxpayers was not used as basis; a more reliable tax mapping is needed.
Application for title could not be done due to lack of documents; Municipal Assessor to prioritize filing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Vice Mayor, as representative of the Municipality of Bindoy, request the treasurer of the Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines to acknowledge its payment (as well as collections from other members) with government accountable form No. 51 in order that the funds may be subject to government accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality requested the representative of the Vice Mayor’s League to acknowledge its payment as required; representative of the organization was referred to the Provincial Vice-Mayor’s League.
observations.reason: Issuance of said Official Receipt will still be referred to the Provincial Vice-Mayor's League.

observations.recommendation: Require the Project Engineer and the Municipal Engineer to submit the Revised Program of Work and As-built plans and a written justification for the implementation of the project that is not in accordance with the corresponding approved plans and program of work.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was not able to prepare the Revised Program of Work and As-built plans and drawings because the programs of work and as-built plans of current projects were not prioritized; Programs of Work and As-built plans of the project were prioritized subsequently.
observations.reason: Preparation of Programs of Work and As-built plans was not prioritized; some actions were taken subsequently but documentations were not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Immediately require all collecting officers including all field collectors to maintain and update the entries in their cashbooks where all their transactions will be recorded pursuant to the above-cited provisions on the Handbook on Cash Examination; and b. Effect regular quarterly reconciliation between the cashbooks to be maintained by the collecting officers and the subsidiary ledgers kept by the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: The municipality directed collecting officers to maintain cashbooks and to effect regular reconciliations with the Accounting Unit. The collecting officers did not fully implement maintenance of cashbooks earlier but submissions and reconciliations improved subsequently.
observations.reason: Some collecting officers did not maintain cashbooks regularly although the Municipal Treasurer receives reports and collections turned over; full compliance remains outstanding.

observations.recommendation: Require the officials and other personnel concerned to refund immediately and in full the difference between the honorarium they received from the local government unit and the reasonable cost of meals and transportation allowance to which they are entitled under COMELEC Resolution dated March 27, 2007. Henceforth, the LGU should no longer appropriate out of its own funds election expenses that should be borne by the COMELEC as provided for in Omnibus Election Code unless there is a COMELEC directive or a valid agreement between the two parties for the LGU to advance the amount first to be reimbursed by the COMELEC later.
observations.managementAction: None recorded in the table for this item; the officials and other personnel concerned refused to refund on the basis that they still need to verify from COMELEC regarding their honoraria for the services they rendered during the May 14, 2007 elections.
observations.reason: Officials and personnel concerned refused to refund pending verification from COMELEC regarding their honoraria.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU management evaluate and take into consideration the advantages in creating an Internal Audit Service, so that management may achieve efficient and effective fiscal administration and performance of its affairs and functions.
observations.managementAction: The IAS was created about 10 years ago but its functions were not specifically defined and it was not functional; some members had retired. The Municipal Mayor commented that he will be designating someone to perform the IAS functions in CY 2020.
observations.reason: The IAS was created previously but was not functional; members retired and functions were not defined. Municipal Mayor to designate personnel in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to order the withholding of
payment of any money due to officials and employees with unliquidated cash
advances thirty days after the service of the demand letters where no liquidation
has been submitted by the accountable officers concerned, pursuant to COA
Circular No. 97-002. We further recommended and management also agreed that
management henceforth desist from granting additional cash advances to
accountable officers with unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed that:
a) the Municipal Agriculturist, in coordination with the Municipal
Environment and Natural Resources Officer, conduct an inventory of
breeding stocks and plants and trees as of the end of each year and
thereafter render a report including the fair market value of these items
to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of the
LGU;
b) the Municipal Agriculturist henceforth keep a systematic record of the
Municipality’s breeding stocks and trees, reporting births, deaths and
disposal, and in case of death or disposal, facilitate request for relief of
accountability in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992 to appropriately drop the amount from the books;
and
c) the Municipal Accountant disclose the composition of the Biological
Assets account in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance
with PPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect the
fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require all PHWs and
other entitled personnel to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of
actual exposure to specific health hazards and occupational risks, certifications
from appropriate agencies and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of
payments.

We recommended and management agreed that overtime services be authorized
only when extremely necessary and only for activities contemplated under CSC-
DBM Joint Circular No. 02, s. 2015 and COA Circular No. 78-77, should generally

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and 41 of R.A. 9003. Enforce strictly the Municipality’s Ordinances relative to solid waste management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof; and c. Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with R.A No. 9003.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the municipal officials adhere strictly to the
provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 on the
use of the 20% Development Fund in order that the desired socio-economic
and environmental management outcomes will be achieved.

We recommended that the municipality limit the payment of hazard
allowance to days an employee is actually exposed to dangers or occupational
risks due to the type of cases/clientele they actually handled as enumerated
under Section 16.1.1 of the IRR of R.A. 9433, as well as during assignments
to remote and depressed areas, strife-torn or embattled areas, distress or
isolated stations, mental hospitals, leprosaria, areas declared under a state of
calamity or emergency which expose them to great danger, and volcanic
activity/eruption. Moreover, subsistence allowance shall only be given for
continuous service during the day including mealtimes. We further
recommended that claims for hazard allowance be supported with proof of
exposure to risk for each day claimed.

We recommended that the Mayor desist from granting cash advances
for intelligence funds to PNP employees and instead draw the cash advance
himself or designate a Special Disbursing Officer who is a regular/permanent
employee of the municipality.

We recommended that management:
a) Prioritize the allocation of funds for the establishment and
operation of a sanitary landfill in accordance with Sections 40 and
41 of R.A. 9003. In the meantime, take action to convert the
existing open dump site into a controlled dump and hasten its
closure within a specific time frame;
b) Enforce strictly the Municipality’s Ordinances relative to solid
waste management and ensure 100 percent compliance thereof;
and
c) Involve all barangays to actively participate in the implementation
of waste reduction and segregation at source in accordance with
RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor determine the person
responsible for the renewal of registration of motor vehicles and require
him/her to refund immediately the corresponding penalties erroneously
charged to government funds. We further recommended that said personnel
be required to henceforth monitor the renewal dates of registration of all
motor vehicles to ensure that these are paid on time.

We recommended that administrative costs be charged against the
LGU’s counterpart fund for the implementation of the SALINTUBIG
project as stipulated in the MOA between the Municipality and the DILG.

We recommended that the municipality appropriate in its annual
budget at least 1% of its internal revenue allotment for the operation of the
Local Council for the Protection of Children. We further recommended that
the municipal Mayor create the Inter-Agency Monitoring Task Force in
order to monitor and evaluate the functionality of the LCPC. Lastly, we
recommend that the LCPC be required to submit its Annual Work and
Financial Plan for incorporation in the Local Investment Plan/Annual
Investment Program, as well as all other reports required under DILG MC
No. 2008-136.

We recommended the imposition of sanctions as provided under Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 specifically the filing of malversation charges and appropriate administrative proceedings against the erring accountable officers. We further recommended that henceforth, municipal officials who issue clearance, authorize and participate in the release of final amounts due officials who retire, die, separate or transfer see to it that unliquidated cash advances are deducted from their claims.
observations.managementAction: Some officers and employees have settled their unliquidated cash advances upon retirement.

observations.recommendation: Required the municipal treasurer to prepare a certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of real property taxes due and collectible for the year to serve as basis for the municipal accountant in recording the Real Property Tax Receivables and their corresponding credits at the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.reason: The municipality acquired the ITAX Program of the Provincial Government that would generate the annual real property taxes due and collectible.  However, no property taxes receivable were recognized in the books.
